Practice: Grouped Distributions

Goal

Practice comparing continuous distributions across groups.

Load Data

nhanes_path <- "data/real/nhanes_2021_2023_analysis.csv"

nhanes <- read_csv(nhanes_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

nhanes |>
  glimpse()
Rows: 11,933
Columns: 57
$ respondent_id                 <dbl> 130378, 130379, 130380, 130381, 130382, …
$ release_cycle                 <dbl> 12, 12, 12, 12, 12, 12, 12, 12, 12, 12, …
$ interview_exam_status         <chr> "interviewed_and_examined", "interviewed…
$ gender                        <chr> "male", "male", "female", "female", "mal…
$ age_years                     <dbl> 43, 66, 44, 5, 2, 3, 43, 65, 34, 68, 27,…
$ age_months_screening_0_to_24  <dbl> NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, NA, …

$ day2_energy_kcal              <dbl> 1040, 2228, 1475, 1068, 1067, NA, NA, NA…

$ day2_total_sugars_g           <dbl> 0.41, 113.41, 107.18, 59.36, 55.13, NA, …

$ day2_sodium_mg                <dbl> 1660, 3438, 2979, 1711, 1770, NA, NA, NA…

Part 1: Prepare Data

adult_bp <- nhanes |>
  filter(
    age_years >= 18,
    !is.na(systolic_mean),
    !is.na(age_group)
  )

adult_bp |>
  count(age_group)
# A tibble: 5 × 2
  age_group     n
  <chr>     <int>
1 18-29       882
2 30-44      1298
3 45-64      2005
4 65-79      1618
5 80+         320

Why is it useful to count rows by group before comparing distributions?

TODO: Replace this with your answer.

Part 2: Box Plot

p1 <- ggplot(adult_bp, aes(x = age_group, y = systolic_mean))

p1
p2 <- p1 +
  geom_boxplot()

p2
p3 <- p2 +
  labs(
    title = "Adult Systolic Blood Pressure By Age Group",
    x = "Age group",
    y = "Mean systolic blood pressure"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p3

What differs across age groups: center, spread, shape, outliers, or overlap?

TODO: Replace this with your answer.

Part 3: Add Points

p1 <- ggplot(adult_bp, aes(x = age_group, y = systolic_mean)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA)

p1
p2 <- p1 +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.15, size = 0.8)

p2

What did the points add? Did they also add clutter?

TODO: Replace this with your answer.

Part 4: Violin Plot

p1 <- ggplot(adult_bp, aes(x = age_group, y = systolic_mean))

p1
p2 <- p1 +
  geom_violin(fill = "gray85")

p2
p3 <- p2 +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.12, outlier.shape = NA)

p3

Which plot works better for this question: box plot, points, or violin? Why?

TODO: Replace this with your answer.

Part 5: Group Summaries

age_group_summary <- adult_bp |>
  group_by(age_group) |>
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_systolic = mean(systolic_mean),
    median_systolic = median(systolic_mean),
    iqr_systolic = IQR(systolic_mean),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

age_group_summary
# A tibble: 5 × 5
  age_group     n mean_systolic median_systolic iqr_systolic
  <chr>     <int>         <dbl>           <dbl>        <dbl>
1 18-29       882          112.            111          14.6
2 30-44      1298          115.            114.         16.7
3 45-64      2005          124.            123.         20.7
4 65-79      1618          129.            127          24.7
5 80+         320          135.            134          29.0

Write a short interpretation using both the plot and the summary table.
